data.description:
## What
The three-pass final-gate run on epic 5fd2 (its own authoritative spec, all overlays) surfaced — and a separate verifier confirmed (advisory, confidence .71, severity critical) — a real T12 rollout/rollback gap the five prior review rounds missed: the gate introduces a **blocking change to the in-use `claim` (open->in_progress) path** but never specifies how to **disable/roll back the gate itself**, nor the deploy ordering for the signature/REVIEW_RESULT-sidecar scheme.

## Why
A gate with no off-switch is a single point of failure for every claim across the diverse client base; a botched rollout of the signing scheme could block all claims with no fast revert. The gate's coaching posture + `--force` per-claim is not a system-level kill switch.

## Scope
- A project-level **disable-the-gate** config (gate off => claim behaves exactly as today; fail-open).
- A documented **rollback / back-out** path for enabling the gate (and for the signature scheme), with old+new coexistence during rollout (older clones preserve-and-ignore the new events — cross-check with the TAG_DELTA/event-rollout note).
- Deploy ordering for the REVIEW_RESULT sidecar + signature events vs the claim-path check.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A config flag disables the plan-review gate so `claim` is unchanged (fail-open) when off; covered by a test.
- [ ] A written rollback/back-out plan for enabling the gate and the signature scheme, with deploy-ordering + old/new coexistence documented.
- [ ] The epic 5fd2 spec references this rollout/rollback design (closes the T12 gap the gate flagged on itself).
data.title: Plan-review gate: kill-switch/disable-config + rollout-ordering for the signature scheme (T12 gap the gate found on itself)
